title: Bleasby (3) R.5698 Memorial
crew_summary: 8 killed including Flg Off. Joseph Widger Murphy MacDonald

maintainer.notes: Part of the Bleasby Aircrew Memorial site.

external_narratives.summary: Lancaster R5698 (coded UG:P) of 1654 HCU was on a night training cross-country from RAF Wigsley on 1 September 1943 when it collided head-on at 1,500 feet with Lancaster JB132 of 61 Squadron returning from a Berlin raid. The collision occurred at 03:26 hours, 4 miles NW of RAF Syerston, killing all 8 crew of R5698.
key_details: Collision at 03:26 hours, 4 miles NW of Syerston

external_narratives.summary: The R5698 crew is commemorated on the Bleasby Aircrew Memorial dedicated on 1 September 2018, exactly 75 years after the mid-air collision. The memorial honours 14 airmen from this single collision event.
key_details: Memorial dedicated exactly 75 years after collision

external_narratives.summary: Local newspaper coverage of the memorial dedication ceremony, describing the community-led project to honour the approximately 40 airmen who perished near Bleasby during the war.
